ruling_reference: Option (a) — single analyzer slice (see analysis.json ruling block)
objective: Consolidate rag-index to a single analyzer slice by reading .coderef/graph.json directly instead of running an independent analyzerService.analyze(...). Establish DUAL structural identities — AC-05a element-grain (chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) === graph.json missing-header node count, ~2410===~2410) AND AC-05b file-grain (uniqueFiles(skipped header_missing chunks) === validation_report.header_missing_count, 262===262). Both identities hold by construction post-pivot; AC-05b preserves the validation-report-as-canonical-baseline contract through Phases 0-8. File-grain aggregation lives in TEST CODE only (no IndexingResult schema delta). Net negative LOC (delete ~100, add ~30 src + ~5 test). Kills STUB-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001 via supersession.

acceptance_criteria.description: indexing-orchestrator.ts no longer calls analyzerService.analyze(...) inside indexCodebase. Verified by grep: zero occurrences of `this.analyzerService.analyze` in the file.
indexing-orchestrator.ts reads .coderef/graph.json directly and converts to DependencyGraph shape ChunkConverter consumes. The facet enrichment block (lines ~462-564) is deleted.
acceptance_criteria.verification: grep for the comment 'Phase 7 task 1.6 — facet enrichment from canonical ExportedGraph' → 0 results in src/. The graph.json read happens once, as the chunk source, not as enrichment overlay.

acceptance_criteria.description: If .coderef/graph.json is missing, indexCodebase throws an explicit error: 'graph.json not found — run `coderef populate` before `coderef rag-index`.'
acceptance_criteria.verification: Unit test: invoke indexCodebase against a directory with no .coderef/graph.json; assert thrown error message matches.
acceptance_criteria.halt_if_unreachable: Cannot detect missing graph.json without filesystem access in indexing-orchestrator → HALT (architectural smell)

acceptance_criteria.description: Stale-graph check fires when sampled source files have mtime > graph.json mtime. Decision (warn vs fail) made in Phase 1 and documented in INVESTIGATION-REPORT.md.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Unit test: touch a source file post-graph.json; invoke indexCodebase; assert warning or thrown error per Phase 1 decision.

acceptance_criteria.description: Element-grain structural identity: chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) === count of nodes in graph.json with metadata.headerStatus === 'missing'. Both numbers are element-grain (per CORE Phase 1 probe: ~2410 === ~2410 on coderef-core itself). This identity holds 'by construction' because chunks ARE the nodes after the pivot.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run populate.js + rag-index.js. Compute graph.json missing-header element count (one node per fn/class/method with headerStatus='missing'). Assert exactly equal to IndexingResult.chunksSkipped count where reason === 'header_status_missing'.
acceptance_criteria.halt_if_unreachable: If element-grain identity fails → HALT (likely a propagation bug from deleting the facet enrichment block; ChunkConverter must propagate node.metadata.headerStatus → chunk.headerStatus inline)

acceptance_criteria.description: File-grain cross-component identity: uniqueFiles(chunksSkippedDetails where reason === 'header_status_missing').size === validation_report.header_missing_count exactly. Both numbers are file-grain (262 === 262 on coderef-core itself). This identity preserves the validation-report-as-canonical-baseline contract that has been load-bearing through Phases 0-8 — the cross-component invariant connecting upstream pipeline output to downstream indexing consumer.
acceptance_criteria.verification: After indexing run, compute new Set(IndexingResult.chunksSkippedDetails.filter(s => s.reason === 'header_status_missing').map(s => fileOf(s.coderefId))).size. Assert exactly equal to validation_report.header_missing_count. NO ±10% tolerance band — strict identity.
acceptance_criteria.halt_if_unreachable: If file-grain identity fails → HALT (the file populations have diverged, which after the single-slice pivot should be structurally impossible; investigate root cause before merge)
acceptance_criteria.implementation_note: Aggregation lives in TEST CODE only. Counting uniques over the existing chunksSkippedDetails array introduces zero IndexingResult schema delta (DR-PHASE-7-B preserved). The test assertion specifies the contract; no runtime aggregation seam.

acceptance_criteria.description: Zero changes outside src/integration/rag/*. Verified by git diff --stat.
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --stat <base>..HEAD -- src/ shows ONLY src/integration/rag/* paths.

acceptance_criteria.description: Zero new fields on IndexingResult schema. Zero new SkipReason enum values.
acceptance_criteria.verification: grep IndexingResult shape pre/post → identical fields. grep SkipReason enum pre/post → identical values.

acceptance_criteria.description: tsc --noEmit clean on both tsconfig.json and tsconfig.cli.json after each phase commit.
Predecessor + Phase 7 archives untouched. Superseded WO directory (rag-index-analyzer-slice-coverage) status block updated but content otherwise preserved.
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff <base>..HEAD -- coderef/archived/{indexing-orchestrator-path-normalization-fix,pipeline-indexing-rag}/ → EMPTY. coderef/workorder/rag-index-analyzer-slice-coverage/ shows only communication.json status flip.

acceptance_criteria.description: Net code reduction: lines deleted > lines added in src/integration/rag/* (excluding tests).
acceptance_criteria.verification: git diff --stat <base>..HEAD -- src/integration/rag/*.ts (excluding __tests__/) → net negative LOC.

acceptance_criteria.description: WO archived under coderef/archived/rag-index-single-analyzer-slice/ with ARCHIVED.md including AC table + ruling rationale + commit chain + superseded-WO note.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Cross-project /close-workorder run by SKILLS produces ARCHIVED.md.

phases.label: Investigation — verify shape compatibility + downstream-consumer audit

tasks.description: Read ChunkConverter.convertGraph + convertNode (src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts). Document: (a) what shape DependencyGraph it expects; (b) whether convertNode propagates node.metadata.headerStatus to chunk.headerStatus inline; (c) whether convertNode propagates layer/capability/constraints inline.

Read graph-builder.ts to understand DependencyGraph shape (Map<string, GraphNode>, edges array). Compare to graph.json's serialized shape. Decide: do we need a JSON-to-DependencyGraph adapter, or is the JSON shape directly consumable?

Audit callers of IndexingOrchestrator.indexCodebase: grep src/ + __tests__/ for `indexCodebase(`. List all callers; verify none rely on the divergent rag-index analyzer slice (e.g., 'rag-index sees files populate doesn't').
tasks.halt_if: Any caller relies on the divergent slice → HALT, report

tasks.description: Decide stale-graph check: warn vs fail. Document choice in INVESTIGATION-REPORT.md with rationale. Default recommendation: fail loud (DR-SINGLE-SLICE-D). Override only if Phase 1 finds a workflow that legitimately runs rag-index post-edit-pre-populate.
Author INVESTIGATION-REPORT.md in WO directory: shape compatibility findings, ChunkConverter propagation status, callers audit, stale-graph decision.

halt_and_report: 1.3 finds a downstream consumer relying on divergent slice → HALT, report (R-SINGLE-SLICE-4)
1.1/1.2 finds ChunkConverter requires changes outside src/integration/rag/* to consume graph.json → HALT (DR-SINGLE-SLICE-A violation)

phases.label: Implementation — replace analyze call, delete enrichment block, add safeguards

tasks.description: Replace analyzerService.analyze(...) at indexing-orchestrator.ts:391 with a graph.json read. Add JSON-to-DependencyGraph adapter (if Phase 1 found one needed). Pass graph to ChunkConverter as before.
tasks.commit_strategy: atomic commit: feat(WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001): replace analyzerService.analyze with graph.json read

tasks.description: Add explicit error for missing graph.json: 'graph.json not found — run `coderef populate` before `coderef rag-index`.'
tasks.commit_strategy: atomic commit: feat(WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001): explicit error for missing graph.json

tasks.description: Add stale-graph check (warn or fail per Phase 1 decision). Sample N source files; compare mtime to graph.json mtime.
Delete the facet enrichment block (lines ~462-564). If Phase 1 found ChunkConverter does NOT propagate node.metadata.headerStatus inline, add the propagation in convertNode (1-3 lines).
tasks.commit_strategy: atomic commit: refactor(WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001): delete redundant facet enrichment block

tasks.description: Run tsc --noEmit on both configs after each commit.

halt_and_report: Fix would require change outside src/integration/rag/* → HALT (DR-SINGLE-SLICE-A)
Fix would require new IndexingResult field → HALT (DR-PHASE-7-B)
Fix would require new SkipReason value → HALT (out-of-scope)
Phase 1 deferred decision (warn vs fail staleness) reveals it's a hard architectural choice, not a stylistic one → HALT, ORCHESTRATOR rules

phases.label: Tests — staleness + missing-graph + dual-AC identity (AC-05a element-grain + AC-05b file-grain)

tasks.description: Add unit test for missing graph.json error (AC-03). Invoke indexCodebase against a temp dir with no .coderef/graph.json; assert thrown error message.
Add unit test for stale-graph check (AC-04). Touch a source file post-graph.json; invoke indexCodebase; assert warn/error per Phase 1 decision.

Rewrite predecessor invariant test (__tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:102-223 'AC-09 dynamic invariant — chunksSkipped tracks header_missing_count (±10%)'). Replace ±10% file-grain assertion with TWO strict-identity assertions: (a) AC-05a element-grain: chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) === count of graph.json nodes with metadata.headerStatus==='missing' EXACTLY; (b) AC-05b file-grain: uniqueFiles(chunksSkippedDetails where reason==='header_status_missing').size === validation_report.header_missing_count EXACTLY. Drop the ±10% language entirely — that band was the probabilistic concession to two-slice divergence and is no longer the right contract.
tasks.fixture_requirement: CORE flagged: existing fixture has N=10 missing-header files with one node each. Post-pivot, AC-05a and AC-05b collapse to the same number on this fixture. Tweak fixture to have multiple element-grain nodes per file (e.g., 2-3 nodes per file across the 10 files) so AC-05a (~25 element-grain) and AC-05b (10 file-grain) are demonstrably distinct numbers and the dual-AC framing is actually exercised.
tasks.commit_strategy: atomic commit: test(WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001): rewrite indexing-gate-invariant for dual-AC identity (drop ±10% band, add element-grain + file-grain identities, multi-node-per-file fixture)

tasks.description: Run full src/integration/rag/__tests__/ suite. Confirm zero regressions on predecessor WO's chunk-conversion + invariant tests + path-normalization tests.
tasks.verification: Test runner output PASS for all suites

tasks.description: Run __tests__/pipeline/graph-ground-truth.test.ts. Confirm 6/6 PASS.

halt_and_report: Any predecessor test regresses → HALT, investigate (likely a propagation bug from deleting the enrichment block)
AC-05a or AC-05b fixture-level test fails on the rewritten invariant → HALT, investigate which identity is broken (substrate vs propagation)

phases.label: Dynamic re-verify + close

tasks.description: Run populate.js + rag-index.js against coderef-core. Capture: (1) chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) count; (2) graph.json node count where metadata.headerStatus==='missing' (element-grain); (3) uniqueFiles count over chunksSkippedDetails filtered to reason==='header_status_missing' (file-grain); (4) validation_report.header_missing_count (file-grain).
tasks.verification: Both identities hold STRICTLY: AC-05a — (1) === (2) exactly (~2410 === ~2410). AC-05b — (3) === (4) exactly (262 === 262). NO tolerance band on either.
tasks.halt_if: Either identity fails → HALT, investigate. AC-05a failure is propagation bug (chunk.headerStatus not flowing from node.metadata.headerStatus). AC-05b failure is structural impossibility post-pivot — investigate root cause before merge.

tasks.description: Verify net negative LOC (AC-12). Run git diff --stat <base>..HEAD -- src/integration/rag/*.ts excluding __tests__/.
tasks.verification: diff output shows lines deleted > lines added

tasks.description: Verify boundary (AC-06): git diff --stat <base>..HEAD -- src/ shows ONLY src/integration/rag/* paths.
Verify validation-report.header_missing_count = 262 (AC-11).
Verify predecessor + Phase 7 archives untouched + superseded WO directory only has communication.json status flip (AC-10).
Author closeout report in WO directory; status → completing in communication.json. Push all atomic commits in single push.
tasks.commit_strategy: atomic commit: close(WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001): closeout report + AC table

halt_and_report: AC-05 identity check fails → HALT, investigate; AC-09 identity is the load-bearing structural property of this WO

definition_of_done: AC-01..AC-13 all PASS (AC-05 split into AC-05a + AC-05b — both identities hold strictly)
All atomic commits pushed clean to origin/main

execution_rules.atomic_commits: one per phase task per fix target; one push at end of each phase
execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit clean on both configs after each commit
execution_rules.boundary: src/integration/rag/* ONLY for src/ changes; tests in src/integration/rag/__tests__/ and __tests__/pipeline/ only
execution_rules.halt_protocol: if any halt-and-report point trips, status → halted in communication.json, await ORCHESTRATOR ruling before resuming

out_of_scope: Superseded WO content changes (status block update only)
The 5 other CORE bulletproof concepts (frozen fixture, branded paths, schema reduction, seam deletion, two-grain drop) — file as separate stubs, scope as separate WOs
